content.thinking:
Now I can see the current state:

**On /app vhost:**
- admin: `.*`, `.*`, `.*` (full permissions) — that's fine for admin
- ops-user: `.*`, `.*`, `.*` — ops-user should NOT have any permissions on /app
- app-user: `` (empty configure!), `.*`, `.*` — app-user has broken configure permission on /app (empty string instead of `.*`)

**On /ops vhost:**
- admin: `.*`, `.*`, `.*` — fine
- ops-user: no permissions at all — ops-user should have full permissions on /ops

So there are three issues to fix: app-user's configure permission on /app is empty and needs to be `.*`, ops-user has full permissions on /app when they shouldn't have any, and ops-user is missing all permissions on /ops where they need full access. Looking at the pod status, app-client is running but has restarted twice recently, while ops-client is stuck in CrashLoopBackOff.

I need to execute three commands: setting app-user's permissions correctly on /app, removing ops-user's access from /app entirely, and granting ops-user full permissions on /ops.

content.text:
Current state is clear:
- `/app`: app-user has empty configure (broken), ops-user has full permissions (wrong vhost)
- `/ops`: ops-user has no permissions at all (missing)

Applying all three fixes:

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-backup-dr-drill-01-202-61f00901-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /app app-user ".*" ".*" ".*" 2>&1
input.description: Fix app-user configure permission on /app vhost

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-backup-dr-drill-01-202-61f00901-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl clear_permissions -p /app ops-user 2>&1
kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-backup-dr-drill-01-202-61f00901-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /ops ops-user ".*" ".*" ".*" 2>&1
input.description: Grant ops-user full permissions on /ops vhost

content.thinking: ops-client is now running and healthy. app-client is still crashing. Let me check the app-client logs to see what's happening.

content.text: ops-client is healthy. app-client is still crashing — let me check its logs:

content.content: app-client: failed to declare queue in /app: {"error":"bad_request","reason":"inequivalent arg 'x-queue-mode' for queue 'app-queue' in vhost '/app': received none but current is the value 'lazy' of type 'longstr'"}

tool_use_result.stdout: app-client: failed to declare queue in /app: {"error":"bad_request","reason":"inequivalent arg 'x-queue-mode' for queue 'app-queue' in vhost '/app': received none but current is the value 'lazy' of type 'longstr'"}

content.thinking:
The app-client is failing because it's trying to declare a queue `app-queue` in vhost `/app`, but the queue already exists with `x-queue-mode=lazy` set on it, and the client is trying to declare it without that argument. This is a queue argument mismatch issue, not a permissions issue.

The client is trying to declare `app-queue` without `x-queue-mode=lazy`, but the queue already exists with `x-queue-mode=lazy`. The client gets an "inequivalent arg" error. 

I have two options:
1. Delete the existing queue and let the client re-declare it without `x-queue-mode`
2. Check the client's configuration and see if the queue mode needs to be set

I'll use rabbitmqctl to delete the queue and let it be recreated by the client, then examine the app-client deployment to understand what configuration it expects.

content.text: The app-client is hitting a queue argument mismatch — `app-queue` exists with `x-queue-mode=lazy` but the client declares it without that flag. Let me inspect the queue and client config:

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-backup-dr-drill-01-202-61f00901-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_queues -p /app name arguments 2>&1
input.description: List queues and arguments on /app vhost

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"app-client","namespace":"karma-rabbitmq-backup-dr-drill-01-202-61f00901-cluster-a"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"app-client"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"app-client"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","set -e\nbase=\"http://rabbitmq:15672/api\"\nvhost=\"%2Fapp\"\nqueue=\"app-queue\"\nauth=\"${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}\"\nfor i in $(seq 1 60); do\n  if curl -s -u \"$auth\" \"$base/vhosts\" \u003e/dev/null; then\n    break\n  fi\n  sleep 2\ndone\npayload='{\"durable\":true,\"auto_delete\":false,\"arguments\":{\"x-queue-type\":\"classic\"}}'\ncode=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w \"%{http_code}\" -u \"$auth\" \\\n  -H \"content-type: application/json\" \\\n  -XPUT \"$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}\" -d \"$payload\")\nif [ \"$code\" -ge 300 ]; then\n  echo \"app-client: failed to declare queue in /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)\"\n  exit 1\nfi\ni=0\nwhile true; do\n  body='{\"properties\":{\"delivery_mode\":2},\"routing_key\":\"'\"${queue}\"'\",\"payload\":\"app-msg-'\"${i}\"'\",\"payload_encoding\":\"string\"}'\n  code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w \"%{http_code}\" -u \"$auth\" \\\n    -H \"content-type: application/json\" \\\n    -XPOST \"$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish\" -d \"$body\")\n  if [ \"$code\" -ne 200 ]; then\n    echo \"app-client: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)\"\n    exit 1\n  fi\n  i=$((i+1))\n  sleep 2\ndone\n"],"env":[{"name":"APP_USER","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"username","name":"rabbitmq-app"}}},{"name":"APP_PASS","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"rabbitmq-app"}}}],"image":"curlimages/curl:8.6.0","name":"client"}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-03T13:39:16Z"
  generation: 1
  name: app-client
  namespace: karma-rabbitmq-backup-dr-drill-01-202-61f00901-cluster-a
  resourceVersion: "2024749"
  uid: 2d5ff414-c4c2-4ff9-b631-d601bb1b3698
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app-client
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: app-client
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          base="http://rabbitmq:15672/api"
          vhost="%2Fapp"
          queue="app-queue"
          auth="${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}"
          for i in $(seq 1 60); do
            if curl -s -u "$auth" "$base/vhosts" >/dev/null; then
              break
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          payload='{"durable":true,"auto_delete":false,"arguments":{"x-queue-type":"classic"}}'
          code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
            -H "content-type: application/json" \
            -XPUT "$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}" -d "$payload")
          if [ "$code" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "app-client: failed to declare queue in /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)"
            exit 1
          fi
          i=0
          while true; do
            body='{"properties":{"delivery_mode":2},"routing_key":"'"${queue}"'","payload":"app-msg-'"${i}"'","payload_encoding":"string"}'
            code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
              -H "content-type: application/json" \
              -XPOST "$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish" -d "$body")
            if [ "$code" -ne 200 ]; then
              echo "app-client: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)"
              exit 1
            fi
            i=$((i+1))
            sleep 2
          done
        env:
        - name: APP_USER
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: username
              name: rabbitmq-app
        - name: APP_PASS
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: rabbitmq-app
        image: curlimages/curl:8.6.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: client
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

content.thinking:
The app-client tries to declare `app-queue` with arguments `{"x-queue-type":"classic"}` but the queue already exists with `[{"x-queue-mode","lazy"},{"x-queue-type","classic"}]`. The `x-queue-mode=lazy` is an extra argument that the client doesn't include, causing the "inequivalent arg" error.

The fix is to delete the existing queue (which has `x-queue-mode=lazy`) so that the client can re-declare it with just `x-queue-type=classic`. This is a pre-existing queue with a wrong argument set up by the scenario.
I can remove this queue using the RabbitMQ management API. I'll use the HTTP API with curl to delete it, passing the admin credentials to authenticate.

content.text: The existing `app-queue` has an extra `x-queue-mode=lazy` argument that the client doesn't declare, causing a conflict. I need to delete the queue so the client can re-declare it correctly:

input.command:
ADMIN_USER=$(kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-backup-dr-drill-01-202-61f00901-cluster-a get secret rabbitmq-admin -o jsonpath='{.data.username}' | base64 -d)
ADMIN_PASS=$(kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-backup-dr-drill-01-202-61f00901-cluster-a get secret rabbitmq-admin -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
echo "user: $ADMIN_USER"
kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-backup-dr-drill-01-202-61f00901-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl delete_queue app-queue --vhost /app 2>&1

echo ""
echo "=== /ops permissions ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /ops
echo ""

kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl cluster_status --formatter compact 2>&1 | head -5
